task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged in as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a logged-in user indicator showing 'admin' or a successful login dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the Patient Identifier Types management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page header, menu item, or breadcrumb indicating 'Manage Patient Identifier Types' or an 'Add Patient Identifier Type' form.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct Name and Description are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for form fields where 'Name' is filled with 'National ART Number' and 'Description' is filled with 'Government issued Anti-Retroviral Therapy ID'.

task_completion.description: Verify the minimum and maximum length constraints are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for form fields for minimum length containing '4' and maximum length containing '15'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Required and Unique properties are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Required' checkbox or dropdown set to unchecked/No/False, and a 'Unique' checkbox or dropdown set to checked/Yes/True.

task_completion.description: Verify the new Patient Identifier Type was successfully saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success message (e.g., 'Saved successfully') or the 'National ART Number' appearing in the list/table of existing Patient Identifier Types.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a web browser with the application UI being interacted with, not terminal commands, curl requests, or API clients.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction is with the genuine application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should look like a complete web application (e.g., OpenMRS or similar EMR), not a text editor or a locally mocked HTML file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of Python scripts, SQL queries, or automated testing frameworks being used to insert the record directly into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Patient Identifier Type details are: Name: 'National ART Number', Description: 'Government issued Anti-Retroviral Therapy ID', Minimum Length: 4, Maximum Length: 15, Required: False (No), Unique: True (Yes).
